full_text:
1 
 t-CyCIF protocol 
Day 0 
Preparations 
● Make sure that the tissue section being stained is not older than 6 months and has been stored at 
+40C. 
● Set the oven’s temperature to 560C (double check the temperature using a thermometer). 
● Check the levels of xylene and alcohol in the dewaxing and rehydration series in the fume hood, if the 
levels are too low add more xylene or respective alcohol. Prepare fresh xylene and alcohol series after 
using them 5 times. 
● Make sure that all the buffers and antibodies are available: antigen retrieval (100mM citric acid pH 
6.0), odyssey blocking buffer, 30% H202, & 480mM NaOH, and 1x PBS. 
 
Day 1 (approx. 6.5 h) 
1. Dewaxing and antigen retrieval (approx. 2h 10 min) 
Baking: place the slides vertically into a grey plastic slide holder and bake in a 560C oven for 30 min to 1h. 
Note: If the slides are coated with wax for long term storage then keep it longer in Xylene. Start with 10 mins 
3X times Xylene instead of 5mins 3 X xylene. If still you see wax remaining in the slide dip for 10 more min in 
Xylene 
1.1. Deparaffinization + rehydration: 
3 x 5 min Xylene 
1 x 3 min 100% ethanol 
1 x 3 min 90% ethanol 
1 x 3 min 70% ethanol 
1 x 3 min 50% ethanol 
1 x 3 min 30% ethanol 
2 x 1 min successive 1x PBS solution 
 
1.2. Antigen retrieval: 
Always use freshly prepared citric acid buffer, 10 mM, pH 6.0:

nol 
1 x 3 min 50% ethanol 
1 x 3 min 30% ethanol 
2 x 1 min successive 1x PBS solution 
 
1.2. Antigen retrieval: 
Always use freshly prepared citric acid buffer, 10 mM, pH 6.0: 
From 100mM stock 🡪 25mL of stock solution + 225mL of MQ. 100 
mM citric acid 1L: 
● Dissolve 21.0 grams of citric acid monohydrate (Sigma #C1909) to 800 mL of MQ H20. 
● Add 9.94 grams of NaOH pellets (Merck # 1310 -73-2) 
● Leave on a magnetic stirrer to thoroughly mi 
● Measure the pH and set to 6.0 
● Level the volume up to 1L with MQ H20. 
Note: Use the solution not more than 6 months after preparation. Transfer the slides onto a 
white plastic holder and in a glass dish - fill with 250 mL citric acid buffer. 
Boil sections in a microwave at 600 W as follows: 
1. Boil for 4 min - leave the slides inside for 5 min. 
2. Boil for 2 min - leave the slides inside for 5 min. 
3. Boil for 2 min. 
4. Let to cool down for 20 mins RT

2 
 Wash three times with 1x PBS, ~ 3 min each. 
 
 
 
2. Pre -staining (approx. 2h) 
Note: Odyssey buffer should be f iltered each time we make an aliquot. 
 
2.1. Prepare a moist incubation chamber to minimize evaporation: Wet regular tissue paper under the tap water 
and fold it to fit inside the grey slide box. After cooling and washing from step 1.2., take out the slides 
one at a time, dry the surroundings of the tissue section using soft fiber -free tissue paper. Without 
letting the tissue dry, quickly add 200 -300 uL odyssey blocking buffer per section and incubate for 30 
minutes at Room Temperature. 
 
Note: The use of PAP pen is not needed. Do not spread the blocking buffer with a pipette tip, as you 
might scratch the tissue while doing so . In addition, thorough drying of the surroundings of the tissue 
creates a hydrophobic surface so that the solution does not spread outside the tissue. 
 
2.2. After the incubation, remove the blocking buffer by vertically tapping the slide on a tissue paper. Next, 
pre-stain the slides with secondary antibodies and Hoechst only by adding 200 -300uL of pre -diluted 
antibodies per section. Incubate for 60 min at RT in a moist chamber (from 2.1.) . 
 Prepare cocktail of secondary antibodies and Hoechst in odyssey blocking buffer: 
 488 goat anti -rabbit 1:1000 cat:A11034 Slot:+4C Box1 C2

RT in a moist chamber (from 2.1.) . 
 Prepare cocktail of secondary antibodies and Hoechst in odyssey blocking buffer: 
 488 goat anti -rabbit 1:1000 cat:A11034 Slot:+4C Box1 C2 
 555 goat anti -rat 1:1000 cat:A21434 Slot:+4C Box1 C3 
 647 goat anti -mouse 1:1000 cat:A21235 Slot:+4C Box1 C4 
 HOECHST 1:5000 cat:H3570 Slot:+4C Box1 I9 
 
 
Note: HOECHST is usually diluted from pre -diluted 1:100 in odyssey buffer and used for about a month 
Dilute 1:50 from 1:100 to get final dilution of 1:5000.Hoechst stock in +4 Fridge door. 
 
2.3. Transfer the slides into a vertical grey plastic holder placed in a white plastic container with 1xPBS. 
Wash three times with 1x PBS, ~ 3 min each. 
 
 
3. Bleaching/Fluorophore inactivation (approx. 2h) 
 
3.1. Prepare 50 mL bleaching solution in a falcon tube as follows: 
 40 ml 1xPBS 
 7.5ml 30% H2O2 
 2.5ml 480 mM NaOH 
 
3.1. Pour the bleaching solution into a white slide box lid placed on top of a LED light. 
3.2. Using forceps, transfer the slides into a bleaching solution; make sure that the slides are completely 
immersed in the solution. Place another LED light on top of the slide box (step 3.2). Connect bottom 
and top LED lights to power. 
3.3. Photo bleach the sides for 90 min at RT, during which change the buffer solution after every 30 min.

3 
 NOTE: Only for cycles 0 The rest of the cycles are only 1x after 45 min. 
3.4. Wash four times in PBS, ~ 3 min each. 
 
 
 
4. Staining with primary antibodies 
4.1. Stain with unconjugated primary antibodies o/n at +4 °C diluted in Odyssey solution. If you have 
conjugated of different species to the unconjugated then add at the same time. 
Slide ID 1’ Ab 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Day 2 
5. Staining with secondary antibodies 
5.1. Wash three times with 1x PBS, ~ 3 min each. 
Incubate secondary antibodies 1:2000 and Hoechst 1:10000 in Odyssey buffer for 1 hr RT . 
5.2. Wash three times with 1x PBS x3, ~ 3 min each. 
5.3. Dry the surroundings of the tissue section using soft fiber -free tissue paper. Add 80 -100 uL of 70% 
 glycerol per section and carefully cover with a coverslip, avoiding air bubbles. 
5.4. Scan the slides. 
Note: Volume of the 70% glycerol depends on the size of the tissue, bigger tissues may need more 
than 100ul for example 1cm x 1cm tissue might need 120 ul of glycerol 
 
 
6. Cyclic phase – repeat steps as before 
6.1. Decoverslip using 1x PBS 
6.2. Photobleach for 90 mins, change the solution after 45 mins. 
6.3. Wash four times in PBS, ~ 3 min each. 
6.4. Stain with pre -conjugated antibodies and Hoechst 1:10000 o/n at +4°C. 
6.5. Wash three times with 1x PBS, ~ 3 min each. 
6.6. Mount with 70% glycerol and scan

4
